meaning: n.单数adj.单一的,非凡的,异常的,持异议的
examples: In 'Tom likes jazz', the singular verb 'likes' agrees with the subject 'Tom'.
'I am' is the first person singular of the present tense of the verb 'to be'.
I would be a singular businessman.
Until, eventually, he was ready to show his own singular response to Cezanne's investigations into perspective and abstraction.
To battle to a draw with Maggie Thatcher was in my book a singular accomplishment.
